title: He Must Increase, I Must Decrease
subtitle: John the Baptist yields his glory to Christ at the Jordan, as his disciples murmur

stillness: Be still, and let the noise of jealousy fall away.
Where there is confession of sins, or of divine praises, there Jesus comes.
Let Him come now, and remain, and cleanse you in quiet.
gospel: After this, Jesus and His disciples came into Judea,
and there He stayed with them, and through His disciples He baptized,
and many became believers and His disciples.
Now John also was baptizing at Aenon, near Salim, because there were many waters there.
And John sent to Jesus, to be baptized, those who came to him.
Then a question arose among John's disciples concerning purification.
And they came to John and said to him:
Rabbi, He who was with you beyond the Jordan, to whom you bore witness — behold, He now baptizes, and all come to Him.
John answered: He must increase, but I must decrease.
I am not the Christ, but His messenger;
not the Bridegroom of the Church, but the friend of the Bridegroom.
behold: Behold John baptizing at Aenon, near Salim, where the many waters were.
Behold him sending to Jesus all those who came to him, confirming his testimony of Christ.
Behold his disciples — troubled, moved by envy, zealous without discretion for their master.
They murmur, thinking Christ lessens their master's glory and the authority of his baptism.
They come to John and say: behold, He whom you baptized now baptizes, and all come to Him, and you they leave behind.
As if they said: we are deserted, here is emptiness; the crowds run to Him, of you no one cares, and so your glory is stolen away.
But behold John, wishing to calm their envy and their jealousy, commending Christ, preaching lofty things of Him.
He must increase and be exalted, John says; but I must be lessened and humbled.
As the morning star is dimmed in its brightness when the sun rises,
as the herald's authority fades when the judge arrives, and the legate's office when the prince comes.
consider: Vainglory is the cause of all evils.
This jealousy led John's disciples astray; it does not drive the captured only to malice,
but it stands against the virtues, forcing them to bear labors yet robbing them of the fruits.
Many even among the religious are like these disciples of John,
clinging more to certain opinions because they are the opinions of those they love,
than to the very truth which they abandon.
Yet when two are friends, it is a holy thing to honor truth above them.
And mark this: what John said must be in each one of us.
Christ must increase in you — that you grow in His knowledge and His love;
for the more you perceive Him by knowing and by loving, the more Christ increases in you,
as one who grows in seeing the one same light judges that light to be increasing.
And you who so advance must be lessened in your own esteem;
for the more a man knows of the divine height, the smaller he reckons his own littleness and lowliness.
prayer: teach me the truth of life, of justice, and of doctrine —
of heart, of mouth, and of work —
never to forsake it through fear, or human favor, or worldly regard.
Let no tenderness of carnal fear or love prevail in me
to hinder the perfection of spiritual love.
And because, through the sin of our first parents,
our irascible power has contracted weakness, our rational power darkness, and our concupiscible power a proneness to evil,
give me the Paraclete: a defender against persecutions,
an illuminator against errors, a kindler against concupiscence,
that in all things I may be able to do Your good pleasure, and to keep from offending You. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Illum oportet crescere, me autem minui.
latinJewel.english: He must increase, but I must decrease.
